title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.articleTitle: The Invention of the Printing Press
content.text: The invention of the movable-type printing press in the fifteenth century (0) triggered a revolution in the way information was shared. Before this, books were painstakingly copied by hand, making them (1) expensive and rare. Johannes Gutenberg’s innovation (2) it possible to produce multiple copies of a text quickly and at a much lower cost. This (3) led to a surge in literacy rates across Europe as books became accessible to a wider audience. The rapid (4) of ideas played a crucial role in the development of the Renaissance and the Scientific Revolution. Scholars were now able to (5) their findings more easily, allowing for a faster exchange of knowledge between different regions. The printing press also helped to (6) languages, as written forms became more consistent across printed materials. While some authorities at the time (7) the new technology as a threat to their control over information, its impact was ultimately unstoppable. Today, we can draw many (8) between the printing revolution and the rise of the internet, both of which have fundamentally changed the nature of human communication, proving that the way we record and distribute our thoughts is key to the progress of our entire species.

example.explanation: “Triggered a revolution” is a strong collocation for starting a major change.

questions.explanation: “Prohibitively expensive” means so expensive that it prevents people from buying something.
“Rendered [something] possible” is a formal alternative to ‘made’.
“Inevitably” is used to describe a result that was certain to happen.
“Dissemination of ideas” is a high-level academic collocation for spreading information.
“Publish findings” is the standard term for making scientific or scholarly work public.
“Standardize a language” means to make its grammar and spelling consistent.
“Viewed as” is a common structure for expressing how something was perceived.
“Draw parallels between” is a fixed idiom meaning to find similarities between two things.
